channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Telangana HC denies interim relief to Union Minister's son in POCSO case
item.description: In Hyderabad, the Telangana High Court has refused to grant any interim protection from arrest to Bandi Bageerath, son of Union Minister Bandi Sanjay Kumar, in a POCSO case filed against him. Justice ...

item.title: Elections Alberta investigates exposure of millions of voter records by separatist group
item.description: Court filings from the Court of King’s Bench have revealed more information about Elections Alberta’s investigation into a major voter privacy breach. The breach involved the Centurion Project, a regi...

item.title: Yutte Stensgaard and Her Iconic Roles in 1970s Hammer Horror Films
item.description: Born in 1946, Danish actress and model Yutte Stensgaard gained recognition in British cinema during the late 1960s and early 1970s. She became known for her Nordic beauty, long blonde hair, and captiv...

item.title: Government Approves Bill to Upgrade NEMC into Full Environmental Authority
item.description: The Tanzanian government is taking steps to transform the National Environment Management Council (NEMC) into a fully-fledged authority. The proposed law has already been approved by the government an...

item.title: Suspect in Pangasinan Child Rape Case Apprehended in Manila
item.description: Authorities in Manila have arrested a suspect wanted for the alleged rape of a child with a disability in Pangasinan. The arrest was carried out by the Manila Police District in coordination with the ...

item.title: UK blocks entry to foreign far-right activists ahead of London rally
item.description: The UK government has prevented eleven foreign far-right activists from entering the country to attend a rally organised by anti-Islam campaigner Tommy Robinson. The event, titled Unite the Kingdom, i...

item.title: Ebola outbreak in Congo results in 65 deaths
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo has reported a new Ebola outbreak, which has already claimed the lives of 65 people. Health authorities are actively monitoring the situation and have deployed rapid r...

item.title: Zamfara launches first direct Hajj flight from Gusau International Airport
item.description: Zamfara State don mark big milestone as Gov Lawal flag off the first-ever Hajj flight from Gusau International Airport. Dis inaugural flight waka on Friday, carry total of 415 pilgrims, 288 men and 12...

item.title: Zara Tindall celebrates 45 and maintains key role within the Royal Family
item.description: Zara Tindall, daughter of Princess Anne, celebrates her 45th birthday recognised for her down-to-earth nature and popularity within the Royal Family. Often referred to as the family's 'secret weapon',...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada explains CCTV was kept secure to preserve evidence after Senate shooting
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada clarified his earlier comment about 'hiding' CCTV footage following the recent shooting incident at the Senate. He explained that the remark was intended to ensure the footage ...

item.title: PM Modi's official visit to Netherlands for bilateral discussions
item.description: Unfortunately, the full text of the news article could not be accessed due to a server access restriction. The original article was supposed to report on Prime Minister Narendra Modi's arrival in the ...

item.title: Trump Reveals Details of Discussion with Xi Jinping on US Defence of Taiwan
item.description: During his recent visit to China, former US President Donald Trump disclosed important aspects of his conversations with Chinese President Xi Jinping. According to Trump, Xi directly inquired whether ...

item.title: Vitamin B2 Supports Cancer Cells by Protecting Them from Ferroptosis, Study Finds
item.description: Researchers at the University of Würzburg have found that vitamin B2, also known as riboflavin, may inadvertently help cancer cells survive. While essential for human health and normally protective ag...

item.title: Pike River: A Powerful Drama About Families Seeking Justice After a Mining Tragedy
item.description: Pike River, directed by Robert Sarkies and written by Fiona Samuel, is a compelling drama based on the 2010 mining disaster on New Zealand's South Island. The film centres on two mothers, portrayed by...

item.title: Perfume Genius to extend ‘Duo Tour’ with shows in North America and London
item.description: Perfume Genius, the stage name of Mike Hadreas, has revealed an extension to his ‘Duo Tour’, adding new dates in both North America and the UK. Earlier this year, Hadreas performed alongside his longt...

item.title: Republican Strategist Says Jim Jordan's Attempt to Shift Focus from Gas Prices May Backfire
item.description: Amid rising gas prices in the United States, Republican Representative Jim Jordan (R-OH) suggested on CNN that voters should focus more on Iran's nuclear capabilities than the cost of fuel. This remar...

item.title: Keir Starmer’s Leadership Challenges and the Future of Labour
item.description: Keir Starmer, who led Labour to a landslide victory in 2024, faces a probable leadership challenge less than two years into his premiership. Despite winning on an anti-Tory wave, Starmer struggled to ...

item.title: Human Rights Watch documents abuses by M23 rebels and Rwandan army in Uvira, DRC
item.description: Human Rights Watch (HRW) has released a report detailing serious human rights violations carried out by the M23 rebel group and elements of the Rwandan army during a brief occupation of Uvira in easte...

item.title: Colorado GOP County Chair Arrested for Attempting to Solicit a Minor
item.description: Hunter Rivera, the 24-year-old chairman of the Weld County Republican Party in Colorado, was arrested on multiple felony charges related to attempting to solicit a child prostitute. Authorities charge...

item.title: Taxi Fares in South Africa to Increase from 18 May Due to Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: From Monday, 18 May 2026, commuters in South Africa will face an increase in taxi fares, ranging from R2 to R5 on certain routes, as confirmed by the Cape's main taxi associations, CATA and CODETA. Th...
